html:
𐤉𐤄𐤅𐤄

THE ANCIENT COVENANT RECORD

Volume One — Part Three

The Torah — First Book

BERESHIT

In the Beginning

Chapters 26 through 36

Yitzhak — Yaakov — The Twelve Tribes Begin

PRIMARY MANUSCRIPT AUTHORITIES

4QGena (4Q1) · 4QGenb (4Q2) · 4QGenc (4Q3) · 4QGend (4Q4)

4QGene (4Q5) · 4QGenf (4Q6) · 4QGenh (4Q8) · 4QGenj (4Q10)

1QapGen — Genesis Apocryphon — Cave 1 — Aramaic expansion

Ethiopian Orit Ge'ez — Beta Israel Ancient Canon

COMPARATIVE NOTES AT END OF EVERY CHAPTER

DSS · ORIT GE'EZ · MASORETIC VARIANT

YHWH / 𐤉𐤄𐤅𐤄 and The Creator only — no substitutions

All personal names in original Hebrew form throughout

THE COVENANT PASSES TO YITZHAK — CHAPTERS 26 THROUGH 28

BERESHIT — CHAPTER 26 Yitzhak in Gerar — The Covenant Confirmed to Yitzhak

26:1 Now there was a famine in the land, besides the former famine that was in the days of Avraham. And Yitzhak went to Gerar to Avimelekh king of the Philistines.

26:2 And YHWH appeared to him and said: Do not go down to Egypt — dwell in the land of which I shall tell you.

26:3 Sojourn in this land, and I will be with you and will bless you, for to you and to your offspring I will give all these lands, and I will establish the oath that I swore to Avraham your father.

26:4 I will multiply your offspring as the stars of heaven and will give to your offspring all these lands. And in your offspring all the nations of the earth shall be blessed,

26:5 Because Avraham obeyed my voice and kept my charge, my commandments, my statutes, and my laws.

26:6 So Yitzhak settled in Gerar.

26:7 When the men of the place asked him about his wife, he said: She is my sister, for he feared to say: My wife, thinking: lest the men of the place should kill me because of Rivkah, because she was attractive in appearance.

26:8 When he had been there a long time, Avimelekh king of the Philistines looked out of a window and saw Yitzhak laughing with Rivkah his wife.

26:9 So Avimelekh called Yitzhak and said: Behold, she is your wife. How then could you say: She is my sister? Yitzhak said to him: Because I thought: Lest I die because of her.

26:10 Avimelekh said: What is this you have done to us? One of the people might easily have lain with your wife, and you would have brought guilt upon us.

26:11 So Avimelekh warned all the people: Whoever touches this man or his wife shall surely be put to death.

26:12 And Yitzhak sowed in that land and reaped in the same year a hundredfold. YHWH blessed him,

26:13 And the man became rich, and gained more and more until he became very wealthy.

26:14 He had possessions of flocks and herds and many servants, so that the Philistines envied him.

26:15 Now the Philistines had stopped and filled with earth all the wells that his father's servants had dug in the days of Avraham his father.

26:16 And Avimelekh said to Yitzhak: Go away from us, for you are much mightier than we.

26:17 So Yitzhak departed from there and encamped in the Valley of Gerar and settled there.

26:18 And Yitzhak dug again the wells of water that had been dug in the days of Avraham his father, which the Philistines had stopped after the death of Avraham. And he gave them the names that his father had given them.

26:19 But when Yitzhak's servants dug in the valley and found there a well of spring water,

26:20 The herdsmen of Gerar quarreled with Yitzhak's herdsmen, saying: The water is ours. So he called the name of the well Esek, because they contended with him.

26:21 Then they dug another well, and they quarreled over that also, so he called its name Sitnah.

26:22 And he moved from there and dug another well, and they did not quarrel over it. So he called its name Rehovot, saying: For now YHWH has made room for us, and we shall be fruitful in the land.

26:23 From there he went up to Beer-Sheva.

26:24 And YHWH appeared to him the same night and said: I am The Creator of Avraham your father. Fear not, for I am with you and will bless you and multiply your offspring for my servant Avraham's sake.

26:25 So he built an altar there and called upon the name of YHWH and pitched his tent there. And there Yitzhak's servants dug a well.

26:26 When Avimelekh went to him from Gerar with Ahuzzat his adviser and Phicol the commander of his army,

26:27 Yitzhak said to them: Why have you come to me, seeing that you hate me and have sent me away from you?

26:28 They said: We see plainly that YHWH has been with you. So we said: Let there be a sworn oath between us, between you and us, and let us make a covenant with you,

26:29 That you will do us no harm, just as we have not touched you and have done to you nothing but good and have sent you away in peace. You are now the blessed of YHWH.

26:30 So he made them a feast, and they ate and drank.

26:31 In the morning they rose early and exchanged oaths. And Yitzhak sent them on their way, and they departed from him in peace.

26:32 That same day Yitzhak's servants came and told him about the well that they had dug and said to him: We have found water.

26:33 He called it Shivah — therefore the name of the city is Beer-Sheva to this day.

26:34 When Esav was forty years old, he took Yehudit the daughter of Beeri the Hittite to be his wife, and Basemat the daughter of Elon the Hittite,

26:35 And they made life bitter for Yitzhak and Rivkah.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QGena and 4QGenb preserve portions of Chapter 26. The renewal of the Avrahamic covenant to Yitzhak at Beer-Sheva — verses 26:2-5 and 26:24 — is attested in the Cave 4 manuscripts.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 26 in full. Yovelim 24:1-34 covers Yitzhak's sojourn in the Philistine lands and the rediscovery of his father's wells in considerable detail.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] No significant variants from the DSS in this chapter.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 26:5 — because Avraham obeyed my voice and kept my charge, my commandments, my statutes, and my laws. YHWH confirms the covenant to Yitzhak on the basis of Avraham's obedience. The words used — commandments, statutes, laws — are the full Torah vocabulary applied to Avraham before Sinai. Yovelim makes this explicit throughout: the patriarchs observed the feasts, the Sabbath, the covenant laws, as written on the heavenly tablets. The Torah was not invented at Sinai — it was revealed there. It existed before.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 26:18 — Yitzhak dug again the wells of his father and gave them the names his father had given them. This act is covenant inheritance in physical form. The wells represent Avraham's presence, his work, his covenant relationship with the land. Yitzhak does not rename them. He restores the original names. The covenant passes through memory and naming as much as through blood.

BERESHIT — CHAPTER 27 Yaakov Receives the Blessing — Esav's Grief

27:1 When Yitzhak was old and his eyes were dim so that he could not see, he called Esav his older son and said to him: My son. And he answered him: Here I am.

27:2 He said: Behold, I am old — I do not know the day of my death.

27:3 Now then, take your weapons, your quiver and your bow, and go out to the field and hunt game for me,

27:4 And prepare for me delicious food, such as I love, and bring it to me so that I may eat, that my soul may bless you before I die.

27:5 Now Rivkah was listening when Yitzhak spoke to his son Esav. So when Esav went to the field to hunt for game and bring it,

27:6 Rivkah said to her son Yaakov: I heard your father speak to your brother Esav:

27:7 Bring me game and prepare for me delicious food, that I may eat it and bless you before YHWH before I die.

27:8 Now therefore, my son, obey my voice as I command you.

27:9 Go to the flock and bring me two good young goats, so that I may prepare from them delicious food for your father, such as he loves.

27:10 And you shall bring it to your father to eat, so that he may bless you before he dies.

27:11 But Yaakov said to Rivkah his mother: Behold, my brother Esav is a hairy man, and I am a smooth man.

27:12 Perhaps my father will feel me, and I shall seem to be mocking him and bring a curse upon myself and not a blessing.

27:13 His mother said to him: Let your curse be on me, my son — only obey my voice, and go, bring them to me.

27:14 So he went and took them and brought them to his mother, and his mother prepared delicious food, such as his father loved.

27:15 Then Rivkah took the best garments of Esav her older son, which were with her in the house, and put them on Yaakov her younger son.

27:16 And the skins of the young goats she put on his hands and on the smooth part of his neck.

27:17 And she put the delicious food and the bread, which she had prepared, into the hand of her son Yaakov.

27:18 So he went in to his father and said: My father. And he said: Here I am. Who are you, my son?

27:19 Yaakov said to his father: I am Esav your firstborn. I have done as you told me — now sit up and eat of my game, that your soul may bless me.

27:20 But Yitzhak said to his son: How is it that you have found it so quickly, my son? He answered: Because YHWH your The Creator granted me success.

27:21 Then Yitzhak said to Yaakov: Please come near, that I may feel you, my son, to know whether you are really my son Esav or not.

27:22 So Yaakov went near to Yitzhak his father, who felt him and said: The voice is Yaakov's voice, but the hands are the hands of Esav.

27:23 And he did not recognize him, because his hands were hairy like his brother Esav's hands. So he blessed him.

27:24 He said: Are you really my son Esav? He answered: I am.

27:25 Then he said: Bring it near to me, that I may eat of my son's game and bless you. So he brought it near to him, and he ate. And he brought him wine, and he drank.

27:26 Then his father Yitzhak said to him: Come near and kiss me, my son.

27:27 So he came near and kissed him. And Yitzhak smelled the smell of his garments and blessed him and said: See, the smell of my son is as the smell of a field that YHWH has blessed.

27:28 May The Creator give you of the dew of heaven and of the fatness of the earth and plenty of grain and wine.

27:29 Let peoples serve you, and nations bow down to you. Be lord over your brothers, and may your mother's sons bow down to you. Cursed be everyone who curses you, and blessed be everyone who blesses you.

27:30 As soon as Yitzhak had finished blessing Yaakov, when Yaakov had scarcely gone out from the presence of Yitzhak his father, Esav his brother came in from his hunting.

27:31 He also prepared delicious food and brought it to his father. And he said to his father: Let my father arise and eat of his son's game, that you may bless me.

27:32 His father Yitzhak said to him: Who are you? He answered: I am your son, your firstborn, Esav.

27:33 Then Yitzhak trembled very violently and said: Who was it then that hunted game and brought it to me, and I ate it all before you came, and I have blessed him? Yes, and he shall be blessed.

27:34 As soon as Esav heard the words of his father, he cried out with an exceedingly great and bitter cry and said to his father: Bless me, even me also, O my father.

27:35 But he said: Your brother came deceitfully and has taken away your blessing.

27:36 Esav said: Is he not rightly named Yaakov? For he has cheated me these two times. He took away my birthright, and behold, now he has taken away my blessing. Then he said: Have you not reserved a blessing for me?

27:37 Yitzhak answered and said to Esav: Behold, I have made him lord over you, and all his brothers I have given to him for servants, and with grain and wine I have sustained him. What then can I do for you, my son?

27:38 Esav said to his father: Have you but one blessing, my father? Bless me, even me also, O my father. And Esav lifted up his voice and wept.

27:39 Then his father Yitzhak answered and said to him: Behold, away from the fatness of the earth shall your dwelling be, and away from the dew of heaven on high.

27:40 By your sword you shall live, and you shall serve your brother. But when you grow restless you shall break his yoke from your neck.

27:41 Now Esav hated Yaakov because of the blessing with which his father had blessed him, and Esav said to himself: The days of mourning for my father are approaching — then I will kill my brother Yaakov.

27:42 But the words of Esav her older son were told to Rivkah. So she sent and called Yaakov her younger son and said to him: Behold, your brother Esav comforts himself about you by planning to kill you.

27:43 Now therefore, my son, obey my voice. Arise, flee to Lavan my brother in Haran

27:44 And stay with him a while, until your brother's fury turns away —

27:45 Until your brother's anger turns away from you, and he forgets what you have done to him. Then I will send and bring you from there. Why should I be bereft of you both in one day?

27:46 Then Rivkah said to Yitzhak: I loathe my life because of the Hittite women. If Yaakov marries one of the Hittite women like these, one of the women of the land, what good will my life be to me?

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QGena preserves portions of Chapter 27. The blessing of Yaakov by Yitzhak is attested in the Cave 4 manuscripts.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 27 in full. Yovelim 26:1-35 covers the entire blessing narrative and emphasizes that YHWH had ordained the passing of the blessing to Yaakov — Rivkah acts in accordance with the word YHWH had spoken to her at Yaakov and Esav's birth.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] No significant variants from the DSS in this chapter.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 27:33 — Yitzhak trembled very violently and said: he shall be blessed. Despite the deception, despite the shock, Yitzhak does not retract the blessing. He confirms it irrevocably. In the ancient Hebrew theology of blessing, once spoken, it cannot be recalled. The blessing passed from YHWH's intention declared in 25:23 through Rivkah's plan through Yitzhak's mouth — and it held. The means were flawed. The outcome was YHWH's will from before the birth.

BERESHIT — CHAPTER 28 Yitzhak Sends Yaakov to Paddan-Aram — The Dream at Beit-El

28:1 Then Yitzhak called Yaakov and blessed him and directed him: You must not take a wife from the Kena'ani women.

28:2 Arise, go to Paddan-Aram to the house of Betuel your mother's father, and take as your wife from there one of the daughters of Lavan your mother's brother.

28:3 The Creator Almighty bless you and make you fruitful and multiply you, that you may become a company of peoples.

28:4 May he give the blessing of Avraham to you and to your offspring with you, that you may take possession of the land of your sojournings that The Creator gave to Avraham.

28:5 Thus Yitzhak sent Yaakov away. And he went to Paddan-Aram, to Lavan, the son of Betuel the Aramean, the brother of Rivkah, Yaakov's and Esav's mother.

28:6 Now Esav saw that Yitzhak had blessed Yaakov and sent him away to Paddan-Aram to take a wife from there, and that as he blessed him he directed him: You shall not take a wife from the Kena'ani women,

28:7 And that Yaakov had obeyed his father and his mother and gone to Paddan-Aram.

28:8 So when Esav saw that the Kena'ani women did not please Yitzhak his father,

28:9 Esav went to Yishma'el and took as his wife, besides the wives he had, Mahalat the daughter of Yishma'el, Avraham's son, the sister of Nevayot.

28:10 Yaakov left Beer-Sheva and went toward Haran.

28:11 And he came to a certain place and stayed there that night, because the sun had set. Taking one of the stones of the place, he put it under his head and lay down in that place to sleep.

28:12 And he dreamed, and behold, there was a ladder set up on the earth, and the top of it reached to heaven. And behold, the angels of The Creator were ascending and descending on it.

28:13 And behold, YHWH stood above it and said: I am YHWH, The Creator of Avraham your father and The Creator of Yitzhak. The land on which you lie I will give to you and to your offspring.

28:14 Your offspring shall be like the dust of the earth, and you shall spread abroad to the west and to the east and to the north and to the south, and in you and your offspring shall all the families of the earth be blessed.

28:15 Behold, I am with you and will keep you wherever you go, and will bring you back to this land. For I will not leave you until I have done what I have promised you.

28:16 Then Yaakov awoke from his sleep and said: Surely YHWH is in this place, and I did not know it.

28:17 And he was afraid and said: How awesome is this place. This is none other than the house of The Creator, and this is the gate of heaven.

28:18 So early in the morning Yaakov took the stone that he had put under his head and set it up for a pillar and poured oil on the top of it.

28:19 He called the name of that place Beit-El — but the name of the city was Luz at the first.

28:20 Then Yaakov made a vow, saying: If The Creator will be with me and will keep me in this way that I go, and will give me bread to eat and clothing to wear,

28:21 So that I come again to my father's house in peace, then YHWH shall be my The Creator,

28:22 And this stone, which I have set up for a pillar, shall be The Creator's house. And of all that you give me I will give a full tenth to you.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QGena and 4QGenb preserve portions of Chapter 28. The dream at Beit-El — the ladder with angels ascending and descending — is attested in the Cave 4 manuscripts.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 28 in full. Yovelim 27:19-27 covers Yaakov's departure and the Beit-El dream — emphasizing that the angel of the presence was the one who stood with YHWH above the ladder.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] No significant variants from the DSS in this chapter.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 28:12 — a ladder set up on the earth with its top reaching to heaven, and the angels of The Creator ascending and descending on it. This is the cosmic axis — the point where heaven and earth meet. The DSS community understood Beit-El as a type of the sanctuary — the place where YHWH's presence descends and the prayers of the righteous ascend. The angels ascending first — before descending — indicates they were already on earth carrying prayers upward before bringing YHWH's response back down.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 28:22 — of all that you give me I will give a full tenth to you. Yaakov vows the tithe at Beit-El before he has received a single thing from YHWH in Paddan-Aram. He pledges based on YHWH's word alone. The tithe is not a response to blessing already received — it is a covenant commitment made in faith at the beginning of the journey.

YAAKOV IN PADDAN-ARAM — CHAPTERS 29 THROUGH 31

BERESHIT — CHAPTER 29 Yaakov Arrives at Lavan's House — Leah and Rachel — The Sons Begin

29:1 Then Yaakov went on his journey and came to the land of the people of the east.

29:2 As he looked, he saw a well in the field, and behold, three flocks of sheep were lying beside it, for out of that well the flocks were watered. The stone on the well's mouth was large,

29:3 And when all the flocks were gathered there, the shepherds would roll the stone from the mouth of the well and water the sheep, and put the stone back in its place over the mouth of the well.

29:4 Yaakov said to them: My brothers, where do you come from? They said: We are from Haran.

29:5 He said to them: Do you know Lavan the son of Nahor? They said: We know him.

29:6 He said to them: Is it well with him? They said: It is well — and see, Rachel his daughter is coming with the sheep.

29:7 He said: Behold, it is still high day — it is not time for the livestock to be gathered together. Water the sheep and go, pasture them.

29:8 But they said: We cannot until all the flocks are gathered together and the stone is rolled from the mouth of the well — then we water the sheep.

29:9 While he was still speaking with them, Rachel came with her father's sheep, for she was a shepherdess.

29:10 Now as soon as Yaakov saw Rachel the daughter of Lavan his mother's brother, and the sheep of Lavan his mother's brother, Yaakov came near and rolled the stone from the well's mouth and watered the flock of Lavan his mother's brother.

29:11 Then Yaakov kissed Rachel and wept aloud.

29:12 And Yaakov told Rachel that he was her father's kinsman, and that he was Rivkah's son, and she ran and told her father.

29:13 As soon as Lavan heard the news about Yaakov, his sister's son, he ran to meet him and embraced him and kissed him and brought him to his house. He told Lavan all these things,

29:14 And Lavan said to him: Surely you are my bone and my flesh. And he stayed with him a month.

29:15 Then Lavan said to Yaakov: Because you are my kinsman, should you therefore serve me for nothing? Tell me, what shall your wages be?

29:16 Now Lavan had two daughters. The name of the older was Leah, and the name of the younger was Rachel.

29:17 Leah's eyes were weak, but Rachel was beautiful in form and appearance.

29:18 Yaakov loved Rachel. And he said: I will serve you seven years for your younger daughter Rachel.

29:19 Lavan said: It is better that I give her to you than that I should give her to any other man — stay with me.

29:20 So Yaakov served seven years for Rachel, and they seemed to him but a few days because of the love he had for her.

29:21 Then Yaakov said to Lavan: Give me my wife that I may go in to her, for my time is completed.

29:22 So Lavan gathered together all the people of the place and made a feast.

29:23 But in the evening he took his daughter Leah and brought her to Yaakov, and he went in to her.

29:24 Lavan gave his female servant Zilpah to his daughter Leah to be her servant.

29:25 And in the morning, behold, it was Leah. And Yaakov said to Lavan: What is this you have done to me? Did I not serve with you for Rachel? Why then have you deceived me?

29:26 Lavan said: It is not so done in our place, to give the younger before the firstborn.

29:27 Complete the week of this one, and we will give you the other also in return for serving me another seven years.

29:28 Yaakov did so, and completed her week. Then Lavan gave him his daughter Rachel to be his wife.

29:29 Lavan gave his female servant Bilhah to his daughter Rachel to be her servant.

29:30 So Yaakov went in to Rachel also, and he loved Rachel more than Leah, and served Lavan for another seven years.

29:31 When YHWH saw that Leah was hated, he opened her womb, but Rachel was barren.

29:32 And Leah conceived and bore a son, and she called his name Re'uven, for she said: Because YHWH has looked upon my affliction — for now my husband will love me.

29:33 She conceived again and bore a son, and said: Because YHWH has heard that I am hated, he has given me this son also. And she called his name Shim'on.

29:34 Again she conceived and bore a son, and said: Now this time my husband will be attached to me, because I have borne him three sons. Therefore his name was called Levi.

29:35 And she conceived again and bore a son, and said: This time I will praise YHWH. Therefore she called his name Yehudah. And she ceased bearing.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QGena and 4QGenb preserve portions of Chapter 29. The account of Yaakov in Paddan-Aram and the births of the sons are attested in the Cave 4 manuscripts.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 29 in full. Yovelim 28:1-24 covers Yaakov's years with Lavan and the births of the tribes.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] No significant variants from the DSS in this chapter.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 29:31 — YHWH saw that Leah was hated and opened her womb. The one who is overlooked, unloved, second in her husband's affections — YHWH sees her and acts. This is the pattern throughout the ancient record. Hagar in the wilderness. Leah in her tent. YHWH sees the one that men overlook. The four sons of Leah — Re'uven, Shim'on, Levi, Yehudah — become the foundational tribes of Yisra'EL. The covenant line passes through Yehudah, the fourth son of the wife Yaakov loved less.

BERESHIT — CHAPTER 30 The Birth of the Twelve Tribes — Yaakov's Flocks Increase

30:1 When Rachel saw that she bore Yaakov no children, Rachel envied her sister. She said to Yaakov: Give me children, or I shall die.

30:2 Yaakov's anger was kindled against Rachel, and he said: Am I in the place of The Creator, who has withheld from you the fruit of the womb?

30:3 Then she said: Here is my servant Bilhah — go in to her, so that she may give birth on my behalf, that even I may have children through her.

30:4 So she gave him her servant Bilhah as a wife, and Yaakov went in to her.

30:5 And Bilhah conceived and bore Yaakov a son.

30:6 Then Rachel said: The Creator has judged me, and has also heard my voice and given me a son. Therefore she called his name Dan.

30:7 Rachel's servant Bilhah conceived again and bore Yaakov a second son.

30:8 Then Rachel said: With mighty wrestlings I have wrestled with my sister and have prevailed. So she called his name Naftali.

30:9 When Leah saw that she had ceased bearing children, she took her servant Zilpah and gave her to Yaakov as a wife.

30:10 Then Leah's servant Zilpah bore Yaakov a son.

30:11 And Leah said: Good fortune has come. So she called his name Gad.

30:12 Leah's servant Zilpah bore Yaakov a second son.

30:13 And Leah said: Happy am I. For women have called me happy. So she called his name Asher.

30:14 In the days of wheat harvest Re'uven went and found mandrakes in the field and brought them to his mother Leah. Then Rachel said to Leah: Please give me some of your son's mandrakes.

30:15 But she said to her: Is it a small matter that you have taken away my husband? Would you take away my son's mandrakes also? Rachel said: Then he may lie with you tonight in exchange for your son's mandrakes.

30:16 When Yaakov came from the field in the evening, Leah went out to meet him and said: You must come in to me, for I have hired you with my son's mandrakes. So he lay with her that night.

30:17 And The Creator listened to Leah, and she conceived and bore Yaakov a fifth son.

30:18 Leah said: The Creator has given me my wages because I gave my servant to my husband. So she called his name Yissakhar.

30:19 And Leah conceived again, and she bore Yaakov a sixth son.

30:20 Then Leah said: The Creator has endowed me with a good endowment — now my husband will honor me, because I have borne him six sons. So she called his name Zevulun.

30:21 Afterward she bore a daughter and called her name Dinah.

30:22 Then The Creator remembered Rachel, and The Creator listened to her and opened her womb.

30:23 She conceived and bore a son and said: The Creator has taken away my reproach.

30:24 And she called his name Yosef, saying: May YHWH add to me another son.

30:25 As soon as Rachel had borne Yosef, Yaakov said to Lavan: Send me away, that I may go to my own home and country.

30:26 Give me my wives and my children for whom I have served you, and let me go, for you know the service that I have given you.

30:27 But Lavan said to him: If I have found favor in your sight, I have learned by divination that YHWH has blessed me because of you.

30:28 Name your wages, and I will give it.

30:29 Yaakov said to him: You yourself know how I have served you, and how your livestock has fared with me.

30:30 For you had little before I came, and it has increased abundantly, and YHWH has blessed you wherever I turned. But now when shall I provide for my own household also?

30:43 Thus the man increased greatly and had large flocks, female servants and male servants, and camels and donkeys.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QGena and 4QGenb preserve portions of Chapter 30. The births of the sons are attested in the DSS manuscripts.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 30 in full. Yovelim 28:11-24 lists all twelve sons in their tribal order with notes on the mothers who bore each.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] No significant variants from the DSS in this chapter.

[CRITICAL NOTE] The twelve sons of Yaakov are born through four women — Leah, Rachel, Bilhah, Zilpah — two wives and two servants. The twelve tribes of Yisra'EL do not arise from a single pure lineage. They arise from four women, two of them servants, across years of rivalry and longing. The ancient record does not clean this up. It records it exactly as it happened. The covenant does not require a pristine genealogy. It requires YHWH's choice.

BERESHIT — CHAPTER 31 Yaakov Flees from Lavan — The Covenant at Gal-Ed

31:1 Now Yaakov heard that the sons of Lavan were saying: Yaakov has taken all that was our father's, and from what was our father's he has gained all this wealth.

31:2 And Yaakov saw that Lavan did not regard him with favor as before.

31:3 Then YHWH said to Yaakov: Return to the land of your fathers and to your kindred, and I will be with you.

31:4 So Yaakov sent and called Rachel and Leah into the field where his flock was

31:5 And said to them: I see that your father does not regard me with favor as he did before. But the The Creator of my father has been with me.

31:7 Yet your father has cheated me and changed my wages ten times. But The Creator did not permit him to harm me.

31:10 In the breeding season of the flock I lifted up my eyes and saw in a dream that the goats that mated with the flock were streaked, spotted, and mottled.

31:11 Then the angel of The Creator said to me in the dream: Yaakov. And I said: Here I am.

31:12 And he said: Lift up your eyes and see — all the goats that mate with the flock are streaked, spotted, and mottled, for I have seen all that Lavan is doing to you.

31:13 I am the Creator of Beit-El, where you anointed a pillar and made a vow to me. Now arise, go out from this land and return to the land of your kindred.

31:14 Then Rachel and Leah answered and said to him: Is there any portion or inheritance left to us in our father's house?

31:17 So Yaakov arose and set his sons and his wives on camels.

31:18 He drove away all his livestock, all his property that he had gained, the livestock in his possession that he had acquired in Paddan-Aram, to go to the land of Kena'an to his father Yitzhak.

31:22 When it was told Lavan on the third day that Yaakov had fled,

31:23 He took his kinsmen with him and pursued him for seven days and followed close after him into the hill country of Gilead.

31:24 But The Creator came to Lavan the Aramean in a dream by night and said to him: Be careful not to say anything to Yaakov, either good or bad.

31:29 It is in my power to do you harm. But the The Creator of your father spoke to me last night, saying: Be careful not to say anything to Yaakov, either good or bad.

31:36 Then Yaakov became angry and berated Lavan. Yaakov said to Lavan: What is my offense? What is my sin, that you have hotly pursued me?

31:38 These twenty years I have been with you. Your ewes and your female goats have not miscarried, and I have not eaten the rams of your flocks.

31:41 These twenty years I have been in your house. I served you fourteen years for your two daughters, and six years for your flock, and you have changed my wages ten times.

31:42 If the The Creator of my father, the The Creator of Avraham and the Fear of Yitzhak, had not been on my side, surely now you would have sent me away empty-handed. The Creator saw my affliction and the labor of my hands and rebuked you last night.

31:44 Come now, let us make a covenant, you and I. And let it be a witness between you and me.

31:45 So Yaakov took a stone and set it up as a pillar.

31:46 And Yaakov said to his kinsmen: Gather stones. And they took stones and made a heap, and they ate there by the heap.

31:49 And Mitspah, for he said: YHWH watch between you and me, when we are out of one another's sight.

31:54 And Yaakov offered a sacrifice in the hill country and called his kinsmen to eat bread. They ate bread and spent the night in the hill country.

31:55 Early in the morning Lavan arose and kissed his grandchildren and his daughters and blessed them. Then Lavan departed and returned home.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QGena preserves portions of Chapter 31. Yaakov's departure from Paddan-Aram and the covenant at Gal-Ed are attested in the Cave 4 manuscripts.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 31 in full.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] No significant variants from the DSS in this chapter.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 31:42 — the Fear of Yitzhak. This is one of the ancient divine names used by the patriarchs — Pahad Yitzhak — the Fear of Yitzhak. Not a name for YHWH derived from abstract theology but from lived experience. Yitzhak feared YHWH at the Aqedah. That experience became a name. The patriarchs named YHWH by what they encountered. Avraham named him YHWH Will Provide. Hagar named him You Are a Creator of Seeing. Yitzhak's fear at Moriah became a divine name carried down to his son.

YAAKOV BECOMES YISRA'EL — CHAPTERS 32 THROUGH 36

BERESHIT — CHAPTER 32 Yaakov Prepares to Meet Esav — The Wrestling at the Yabok

32:1 Yaakov went on his way, and the angels of The Creator met him.

32:2 And when Yaakov saw them he said: This is The Creator's camp. So he called the name of that place Mahanaim.

32:3 And Yaakov sent messengers before him to Esav his brother in the land of Seir, the country of Edom,

32:4 Instructing them: Thus you shall say to my lord Esav: Thus says your servant Yaakov, I have sojourned with Lavan and stayed until now.

32:6 And the messengers returned to Yaakov, saying: We came to your brother Esav, and he is coming to meet you, and there are four hundred men with him.

32:7 Then Yaakov was greatly afraid and distressed. He divided the people who were with him, and the flocks and herds and camels, into two camps,

32:8 Thinking: If Esav comes to the one camp and attacks it, then the camp that is left will escape.

32:9 And Yaakov said: O The Creator of my father Avraham and The Creator of my father Yitzhak, YHWH who said to me: Return to your country and to your kindred, that I may do you good —

32:10 I am not worthy of the least of all the deeds of steadfast love and all the faithfulness that you have shown to your servant, for with only my staff I crossed this Yarden, and now I have become two camps.

32:11 Please deliver me from the hand of my brother, from the hand of Esav, for I fear him, that he may come and attack me, the mothers with the children.

32:12 But you said: I will surely do you good, and make your offspring as the sand of the sea, which cannot be numbered for multitude.

32:22 The same night he arose and took his two wives, his two female servants, and his eleven children, and crossed the ford of the Yabok.

32:23 He took them and sent them across the stream, and everything else that he had.

32:24 And Yaakov was left alone. And a man wrestled with him until the breaking of the day.

32:25 When the man saw that he did not prevail against Yaakov, he touched his hip socket, and Yaakov's hip was put out of joint as he wrestled with him.

32:26 Then he said: Let me go, for the day has broken. But Yaakov said: I will not let you go unless you bless me.

32:27 And he said to him: What is your name? And he said: Yaakov.

32:28 Then he said: Your name shall no longer be called Yaakov, but Yisra'EL — for you have striven with The Creator and with men, and have prevailed.

32:29 Then Yaakov asked him: Please tell me your name. But he said: Why is it that you ask my name? And there he blessed him.

32:30 So Yaakov called the name of the place Peni'el, saying: For I have seen The Creator face to face, and yet my life has been delivered.

32:31 The sun rose upon him as he passed Peni'el, limping because of his hip.

32:32 Therefore to this day the people of Yisra'EL do not eat the sinew of the thigh that is on the hip socket, because he touched the socket of Yaakov's hip on the sinew of the thigh.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QGena preserves portions of Chapter 32 including the wrestling at the Yabok. The name Yisra'EL given at Peni'el is attested in the Cave 4 manuscripts.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 32 in full. Yovelim 29:1-13 covers Yaakov's preparation to meet Esav and his prayer.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] No significant variants from the DSS in this chapter.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 32:28 — your name shall no longer be called Yaakov, but Yisra'EL — for you have striven with The Creator and with men, and have prevailed. The name Yisra'EL — Yisra-EL — means he who strives with The Creator or The Creator strives or rules. The nation takes its name from a night of wrestling, from a wound, from refusing to release until a blessing was given. This is the theological identity of the covenant people: they wrestle. They do not receive the blessing passively. They contend until dawn. And YHWH blesses the one who will not let go.

BERESHIT — CHAPTER 33 Yaakov and Esav are Reconciled — Yaakov Arrives in Kena'an

33:1 And Yaakov lifted up his eyes and looked, and behold, Esav was coming, and four hundred men with him. So he divided the children among Leah and Rachel and the two female servants.

33:2 And he put the servants with their children in front, then Leah with her children, and Rachel and Yosef last of all.

33:3 He himself went on before them, bowing himself to the ground seven times, until he came near to his brother.

33:4 But Esav ran to meet him and embraced him and fell on his neck and kissed him, and they wept.

33:5 And when Esav lifted up his eyes and saw the women and children, he said: Who are these with you? Yaakov said: The children whom The Creator has graciously given your servant.

33:8 Esav said: What do you mean by all this company that I met? Yaakov answered: To find favor in the sight of my lord.

33:9 But Esav said: I have enough, my brother — keep what you have for yourself.

33:10 Yaakov said: No, please, if I have found favor in your sight, then accept my present from my hand. For I have seen your face, which is like seeing the face of The Creator, and you have accepted me.

33:11 Please accept my blessing that is brought to you, because The Creator has dealt graciously with me, and because I have enough. Thus he urged him, and he took it.

33:17 But Yaakov journeyed to Sukkot and built himself a house and made booths for his livestock. Therefore the name of the place is called Sukkot.

33:18 And Yaakov came safely to the city of Shechem, which is in the land of Kena'an, on his way from Paddan-Aram, and he camped before the city.

33:19 And from the sons of Hamor, Shechem's father, he bought for a hundred pieces of money the piece of land on which he had pitched his tent.

33:20 There he erected an altar and called it El-Elohe-Yisra'EL.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QGena preserves portions of Chapter 33.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 33 in full.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] No significant variants from the DSS in this chapter.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 33:4 — Esav ran to meet him and embraced him and fell on his neck and kissed him, and they wept. Twenty years of separation, a stolen blessing, a death threat — and Esav runs, embraces, weeps. The reconciliation is complete and unguarded. No negotiation. No conditions. This is the ancient record's demonstration that YHWH's word to Rivkah — the older shall serve the younger — was fulfilled not through violence but through the softening of Esav's heart before Yaakov arrived. Yaakov bowed seven times. Esav ran and embraced. YHWH prepared the meeting before either brother took a single step.

BERESHIT — CHAPTER 34 Dinah — Shechem — Shim'on and Levi

34:1 Now Dinah the daughter of Leah, whom she had borne to Yaakov, went out to see the women of the land.

34:2 And when Shechem the son of Hamor the Hivite, the prince of the land, saw her, he seized her and lay with her and humiliated her.

34:3 And his soul was drawn to Dinah the daughter of Yaakov. He loved the young woman and spoke tenderly to her.

34:4 So Shechem spoke to his father Hamor, saying: Get me this girl for my wife.

34:5 Now Yaakov heard that he had defiled his daughter Dinah. But his sons were with his livestock in the field, so Yaakov held his peace until they came.

34:7 And the sons of Yaakov had come in from the field when they heard it, and the men were indignant and very angry, because he had done an outrageous thing in Yisra'EL by lying with Yaakov's daughter, for such a thing must not be done.

34:13 The sons of Yaakov answered Shechem and his father Hamor deceitfully, because he had defiled their sister Dinah.

34:14 They said to them: We cannot do this thing, to give our sister to one who is uncircumcised, for that would be a disgrace to us.

34:15 Only on this condition will we agree with you — that you will become as we are by every male among you being circumcised.

34:25 On the third day, when they were sore, two of the sons of Yaakov, Shim'on and Levi, Dinah's brothers, took their swords and came against the city while it felt secure and killed all the males.

34:26 They killed Hamor and his son Shechem with the sword and took Dinah out of Shechem's house and went away.

34:30 Then Yaakov said to Shim'on and Levi: You have brought trouble on me by making me stink to the inhabitants of the land, the Kena'ani and the Perizzim. My numbers are few, and if they gather themselves against me and attack me, I shall be destroyed, both I and my household.

34:31 But they said: Should he treat our sister like a prostitute?

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QGena preserves portions of Chapter 34.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 34 in full. Yovelim 30:1-26 gives extensive treatment to the Dinah account — declaring that Shim'on and Levi acted righteously and that any Yisra'ELi man who gives his daughter to a foreigner or takes a foreign woman brings defilement. Yovelim frames their action as covenant zeal. The Torah text itself records both Yaakov's rebuke and the sons' defense without resolving the moral question — both voices are preserved.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] No significant variants from the DSS in this chapter.

[CRITICAL NOTE] The Dinah account is preserved without resolution in the Torah text. Yaakov rebukes Shim'on and Levi for the danger they created. They answer with a counter-question about honor. Neither position is rejected. The tension between Yaakov's prudential concern and the brothers' zeal for their sister's dignity runs unresolved through Chapter 34 and is revisited in Yaakov's deathbed words in Chapter 49. The ancient record preserves moral complexity rather than forcing a clean verdict.

BERESHIT — CHAPTER 35 Beit-El Revisited — Rachel Dies — The Twelve Sons Listed — Yitzhak Dies

35:1 The Creator said to Yaakov: Arise, go up to Beit-El and dwell there. Make an altar there to The Creator who appeared to you when you fled from your brother Esav.

35:2 So Yaakov said to his household and to all who were with him: Put away the foreign gods that are among you and purify yourselves and change your garments.

35:3 Then let us arise and go up to Beit-El, so that I may make there an altar to The Creator who answers me in the day of my distress and has been with me wherever I have gone.

35:4 So they gave to Yaakov all the foreign gods that they had, and the rings that were in their ears. Yaakov hid them under the terebinth tree that was near Shechem.

35:5 And as they journeyed, a terror from The Creator fell upon the cities that were around them, so that they did not pursue the sons of Yaakov.

35:6 And Yaakov came to Luz, that is, Beit-El, which is in the land of Kena'an, he and all the people who were with him,

35:7 And there he built an altar and called the place El-Beit-El, because there The Creator had revealed himself to him when he fled from his brother.

35:9 The Creator appeared to Yaakov again, when he came from Paddan-Aram, and blessed him.

35:10 And The Creator said to him: Your name is Yaakov — your name shall not be called Yaakov anymore, but Yisra'EL shall be your name. So he called his name Yisra'EL.

35:11 And The Creator said to him: I am The Creator Almighty — be fruitful and multiply. A nation and a company of nations shall come from you, and kings shall come from your own body.

35:12 The land that I gave to Avraham and Yitzhak I will give to you, and I will give the land to your offspring after you.

35:13 Then The Creator went up from him in the place where he had spoken with him.

35:14 And Yaakov set up a pillar in the place where he had spoken with him, a pillar of stone. He poured out a drink offering on it and poured oil on it.

35:15 So Yaakov called the name of the place where The Creator had spoken with him Beit-El.

35:16 Then they journeyed from Beit-El. When they were still some distance from Efrat, Rachel went into labor, and she had hard labor.

35:17 And when her labor was at its hardest, the midwife said to her: Do not fear, for you have another son.

35:18 And as her soul was departing — for she was dying — she called his name Ben-Oni, but his father called him Binyamin.

35:19 So Rachel died, and she was buried on the way to Efrat, that is, Bethlehem.

35:20 And Yaakov set up a pillar over her tomb. It is the pillar of Rachel's tomb, which is there to this day.

35:21 Yisra'EL journeyed on and pitched his tent beyond the tower of Eder.

35:22 While Yisra'EL lived in that land, Re'uven went and lay with Bilhah his father's concubine. And Yisra'EL heard of it.

35:23 Now the sons of Leah: Re'uven, Yaakov's firstborn, Shim'on, Levi, Yehudah, Yissakhar, and Zevulun.

35:24 The sons of Rachel: Yosef and Binyamin.

35:25 The sons of Bilhah, Rachel's servant: Dan and Naftali.

35:26 The sons of Zilpah, Leah's servant: Gad and Asher. These were the sons of Yaakov who were born to him in Paddan-Aram.

35:27 And Yaakov came to his father Yitzhak at Mamre, or Kiryat-Arba, that is, Hebron, where Avraham and Yitzhak had sojourned.

35:28 Now the days of Yitzhak were 180 years.

35:29 And Yitzhak breathed his last, and he died and was gathered to his people, old and full of days. And his sons Esav and Yaakov buried him.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QGena and 4QGenb preserve portions of Chapter 35. The confirmation of the name Yisra'EL at Beit-El — verse 35:10 — is attested in the Cave 4 manuscripts. The death of Rachel and the list of the twelve sons are preserved in the DSS fragments.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 35 in full. Yovelim 32:1-34 covers the return to Beit-El, the purging of foreign gods, and the renewal of the covenant in considerable detail.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] No significant variants from the DSS in this chapter.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 35:18 — Rachel named him Ben-Oni — son of my sorrow — as she died. Yaakov renamed him Binyamin — son of the right hand — son of strength. The two names for the same child tell two stories simultaneously. The mother's grief and the father's hope. Both names are preserved in the ancient record. The tribe is called Binyamin — the father's name won out — but the mother's dying word was also written down and kept.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 35:22 — Re'uven went and lay with Bilhah his father's concubine. The Torah states this plainly and moves on. The judgment falls later — in Yaakov's deathbed blessing of Chapter 49 where Re'uven loses the firstborn's double portion because of this act. The ancient record holds accounts open across chapters. Nothing is forgotten. The deed in Chapter 35 has consequences in Chapter 49.

BERESHIT — CHAPTER 36 The Generations of Esav — The Kings of Edom

36:1 These are the generations of Esav, that is, Edom.

36:2 Esav took his wives from the Kena'ani women: Adah the daughter of Elon the Hittite, Oholivamah the daughter of Anah the daughter of Tzivon the Hivite,

36:3 And Basemat, Yishma'el's daughter, the sister of Nevayot.

36:4 And Adah bore to Esav, Elifaz. Basemat bore Re'uel.

36:5 Oholivamah bore Yeush, Yaalam, and Korah. These are the sons of Esav who were born to him in the land of Kena'an.

36:6 Then Esav took his wives, his sons, his daughters, and all the members of his household, his livestock, all his beasts, and all his property that he had acquired in the land of Kena'an. He went into a land away from his brother Yaakov.

36:7 For their possessions were too great for them to dwell together. The land of their sojournings could not support them because of their livestock.

36:8 So Esav settled in the hill country of Seir. Esav is Edom.

36:9 These are the generations of Esav the father of the Edomites in the hill country of Seir.

36:31 These are the kings who reigned in the land of Edom, before any king reigned over the people of Yisra'EL.

36:32 Bela the son of Beor reigned in Edom, the name of his city being Dinhavah.

36:33 Bela died, and Yovav the son of Zerah of Botzrah reigned in his place.

36:34 Yovav died, and Husham of the land of the Temanites reigned in his place.

36:35 Husham died, and Hadad the son of Bedad, who defeated Midyan in the country of Moav, reigned in his place, the name of his city being Avit.

36:36 Hadad died, and Samlah of Masrekah reigned in his place.

36:37 Samlah died, and Sha'ul of Rehovot on the Euphrates reigned in his place.

36:38 Sha'ul died, and Ba'al-Hanan the son of Akhbor reigned in his place.

36:39 Ba'al-Hanan the son of Akhbor died, and Hadar reigned in his place, the name of his city being Pau — his wife's name was Mehetavel, the daughter of Matred, daughter of Me-Zahav.

36:40 These are the names of the chiefs of Esav, according to their clans and their dwelling places, by their names: the chiefs Timnah, Alvah, Yetet,

36:43 Magdiel, and Iram — these are the chiefs of Edom, that is Esav, the father of Edom, according to their dwelling places in the land of their possession.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QGend (4Q4) preserves portions of Chapter 36 — the generations of Esav. The list of Edomite kings is attested in the Cave 4 manuscripts.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 36 in full.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] No significant variants from the DSS in this chapter. The list of Edomite kings in verses 36:31-39 is notable — eight kings ruled in Edom before any king ruled Yisra'EL. The ancient record documents the nations around Yisra'EL with the same precision it documents Yisra'EL itself.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 36:8 — Esav settled in the hill country of Seir. Esav is Edom. The identification of Esav with Edom and the location of his descendants in Seir is the ancient record's geographical anchor for the Edomite nation throughout the prophetic literature. Yesha'yahu, Yirmeyahu, Yehezkel, Ovadyah — all use Edom and Seir as theological categories rooted in this genealogical fact. Esav-Edom-Seir is a single referent in the ancient Hebrew prophetic tradition.

END OF BERESHIT PART THREE — VOLUME ONE

Chapters 26 through 36 — Yitzhak and Yaakov — The Twelve Tribes
